sourceMovie.title: The Post
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Post (2017) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Snowden
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Post clicked for you, Snowden (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Richard Jewell
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Post clicked for you, Richard Jewell (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Post?
faq.answer: Try Snowden, Richard Jewell, JFK—each shares DNA with The Post while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Post?
faq.answer: The Post (2017) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Post worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
